title: The Priestן¿½s Brother
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  Thrice in the night the priest arose
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  From broken sleep to kneel and pray.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  ן¿½Hush, poor ghost, till the red cock crows,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  And I a Mass for your soul may say.ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  Thrice he went to the chamber cold,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  Where, stiff and still uncoffinן¿½d,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  His brother lay, his beads he told,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  And ן¿½Rest, poor spirit, rest,ן¿½ he said.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  Thrice lay the old priest down to sleep
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  Before the morning bell should toll;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  But still he heard-and woke to weep-
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  The crying of his brotherן¿½s soul.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  All through the dark, till dawn was pale,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  The priest tossed in his misery,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  With muffled ears to hide the wail,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  The voice of that ghostן¿½s agony.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  At last the red cock flaps his wings
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  To trumpet of a day new-born.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  The lark, awaking, soaring sings
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  Into the bosom of the morn.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  The priest before the altar stands,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  He hears the spirit call for peace;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  He beats his breast with shaking hands.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  ן¿½O Father, grant this soulן¿½s release.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  ן¿½Most Just and Merciful, set free
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  From Purgatoryן¿½s awful night
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  This sinnerן¿½s soul, to fly to Thee,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  And rest for ever in Thy sight.ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  The Mass is over-still the clerk
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  Kneels pallid in the morning glow.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  He said, ן¿½From evils of the dark
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  Oh, bless me, father, ere you go.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  ן¿½Benediction, that I may rest,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  For all night did the Banshee weep.ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  The priest raised up his hands and blest-
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  ן¿½Go now, my child, and you will sleep.ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  The priest went down the vestry stair,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  He laid his vestments in their place,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  And turned-a pale ghost met him there,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  With beads of pain upon his face.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  ן¿½Brother,ן¿½ he said, ן¿½you have gained me peace,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  But why so long did you know my tears,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  And say no Mass for my soulן¿½s release,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  To save the torture of all those years?ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  ן¿½God rest you, brother,ן¿½ the good priest said,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  ן¿½No years have passed-but a single night.ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  He showed the body uncoffinן¿½d,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  And the six wax candles still alight.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  The living flowers on the dead manן¿½s breast
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  Blew out a perfume sweet and strong.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  The spirit paused ere he passed to rest-
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  ן¿½God save your soul from a night so long.ן¿½
meaning:
Dora Sigerson Shorter's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Priestן¿½s Brother"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
